notes:
Read-only PostgreSQL monitoring feature. internal/query/io.go contains three static, version-branched SQL constants against the system view pg_stat_io plus two pure selector functions (SelectStatIOQuery, SelectStatIOTimeQuery). No security findings.

Assessment of the explicitly requested surfaces:

1. SQL injection (A03 Injection / CWE-89): None. All SQL is compile-time-constant string literals. Version branching switches between fixed constants (PgStatIOPG16 / PgStatIOPG18 / PgStatIOTime) based on an integer version code; no string concatenation of caller data into SQL. The query passes through query.Format (Go text/template), which interpolates only internal Options (NewOptions: pid type, recovery flag, work_mem, schema) and not user-supplied data; this task's queries contain no {{...}} fields at all (verified by Test_SelectStatIOQuery_NoTemplateArtifacts). No user input reaches the query layer.

2. md5() usage (A02 Cryptographic Failures / CWE-328): Not a finding. The io_key = left(md5(backend_type||coalesce(object,'')||coalesce(context,'')),10) is the Postgres SQL md5() function used purely as a row-identity/diff-matching key over system-view dimension values — it is not Go crypto, not a password/token hash, and carries no security property. It mirrors the established statements.go precedent (left(md5(p.userid::text||p.dbid::text||p.queryid::text),10) AS queryid), which uses the identical pattern with no gosec nolint annotation. A gosec-style G401/G501 flag is not warranted: gosec scans Go crypto/md5, not SQL function text in a string literal, and even if surfaced it would be a non-applicable false positive consistent with existing accepted code. No nolint needed for parity with statements.go.

3. Error-path data leakage (A09): None. The selectors return only (query, ncols, diffIntvl) and produce no errors or logs. Tests surface only static query text and column counts; connection/version codes carry no secrets, and no credentials, PII, or query payloads are logged on any path.

4. Dependency safety (A06 Vulnerable Components): No new dependencies introduced. io.go imports nothing; io_test.go uses only already-vendored packages (fmt, strings, testing, internal/postgres, stretchr/testify). No supply-chain delta.

Other OWASP categories (A01 access control, A04 insecure design, A05 misconfiguration, A07 auth, A08 integrity, A10 SSRF) are not applicable to a static read-only query-construction layer with no auth, network egress, deserialization, or external input. No hardcoded secrets detected (the only literals are SQL keywords and pg_stat_io column names).
